records.title: docs_present ignores populated reference_docs trees
records.divergence_description: The Phase 1 runner advertises reference_docs/ as the canonical modern documentation surface, but docs_present() still keys warning behavior only off docs_gathered/.
records.documented_intent: REQ-002 requires modern installs with populated reference_docs/ to count as docs-present input and to suppress legacy code-only warnings.
records.code_behavior: bin/run_playbook.py:1552-1559 checks only docs_gathered/, while the Phase 1 prompt and formal_docs_guard_banner use reference_docs/ as the current contract (bin/run_playbook.py:621-630, 1582-1622, 2241-2246, 2314-2319, 2611-2616).
records.disposition_rationale: The requirement is code-derived from the runner's own modern documentation contract, and the implementation still emits the legacy docs_gathered-only truth signal.
records.proposed_fix: Teach docs_present() to treat reference_docs/ plaintext files as authoritative and reserve docs_gathered/ for legacy supplemental detection. See quality/patches/BUG-001-fix.patch.

records.title: Phase 2 gate accepts line-count placeholders instead of the written exploration contract
records.divergence_description: The executable Phase 2 gate advances on file existence plus a 120-line threshold even when every named exploration heading and content-shape check is absent.
records.documented_intent: REQ-003 requires the exact Phase 2 exploration contract to be enforced before later phases can consume EXPLORATION.md.
records.code_behavior: bin/run_playbook.py:1158-1165 enforces only presence plus line count, ignoring the heading and content checks mechanically enumerated in quality/mechanical/phase2_gate_required_headings.txt:1-8.
records.disposition_rationale: The skill names the closed set of required headings and checks, and the runner currently omits all of them except the length threshold.
records.proposed_fix: Extend check_phase_gate(..., "2") to validate every required heading and content-shape predicate before returning ok=True. See quality/patches/BUG-002-fix.patch.
records.consolidation_rationale: Every absent cell is omitted by the same Phase 2 gate branch in check_phase_gate(), so one gate-enforcement fix closes the entire missing contract set.

records.title: Live INDEX writers collapse non-Code classifications to Code
records.divergence_description: The project-type classifier supports Skill and Hybrid outputs, but both live INDEX.md writers still publish Code unconditionally.
records.documented_intent: REQ-004 requires the exact Code/Skill/Hybrid classification to survive into live run metadata.
records.code_behavior: bin/run_playbook.py:1819-1831 hardcodes "Code" in the stub payload, and bin/run_playbook.py:1888-1895 passes target_project_type="Code" when re-rendering the final INDEX.md.
records.disposition_rationale: The closed-set classifier already exists and downstream tooling depends on the published metadata; hardcoding Code silently erases valid Skill and Hybrid states.
records.proposed_fix: Read the recorded quality/project_type.json classification (or plumb the computed classification through) and use it in both writers. See quality/patches/BUG-003-fix.patch.
records.consolidation_rationale: Both missing cells come from the same hardcoded literal in the stub/final INDEX writers, so one plumbing change restores both Skill and Hybrid.

records.title: Cleanup without PID files widens to workstation-wide pkill patterns
records.divergence_description: When no recorded PID file exists, the runner falls back to pkill -f patterns that can terminate unrelated Claude or Copilot sessions on the same machine.
records.documented_intent: REQ-005 requires cleanup to stay scoped to playbook-owned workers even when PID files are missing.
records.code_behavior: bin/run_playbook.py:2836-2865 defines _pkill_fallback() with broad patterns like "claude -p" and "gh copilot -p", and kill_recorded_processes() unconditionally invokes it when discover_pid_files() returns no files.
records.disposition_rationale: The safe behavior is already present when PID files exist; the fallback path violates the same ownership boundary the feature is supposed to protect.
records.proposed_fix: Replace the pkill fallback with a bounded manual-intervention path (or another ownership-aware mechanism) instead of guessing process ownership from command-line substrings. See quality/patches/BUG-004-fix.patch.

records.title: Installed gate copy omits the citation verifier dependency
records.divergence_description: The setup script installs quality_gate.py into target repos but does not ship the bin/citation_verifier module that the installed gate needs for byte-equality checks.
records.documented_intent: REQ-006 requires installed citable-doc workflows to retain the same Layer-1 byte-equality citation verification as repo-root runs.
records.code_behavior: repos/setup_repos.sh:195-200 copies only .github/skills/quality_gate/quality_gate.py, while the installed gate imports bin.citation_verifier and otherwise warns and skips verification (.github/skills/quality_gate/quality_gate.py:32-44, 1823-1828).
records.disposition_rationale: Installed targets are explicitly advertised as supporting byte-verified citation excerpts; the missing helper turns that guarantee into a silent warning path instead.
records.proposed_fix: Bundle the citation verifier import surface with the installed gate (for example, copy bin/citation_verifier.py and the minimal package scaffold into the target repo). See quality/patches/BUG-005-fix.patch.

records.title: Curated bootstrap docs still present older releases and legacy docs paths as current
records.divergence_description: The self-audit bootstrap packet includes files that describe v1.5.0 or v1.5.1 as current and still teach docs_gathered/ as the documentation path, even though the live repo is v1.5.3 and the current contract is reference_docs/.
records.documented_intent: REQ-007 requires curated bootstrap docs to stay current with the live release and documentation terminology whenever they describe the current product.
records.code_behavior: docs_gathered/01_README_project.md:1-19 still labels the project as v1.5.0 and tells users to create docs_gathered/, while docs_gathered/29_improvement_axes_and_version_history.md:68-72 still labels v1.5.1 as current; the live repo advertises v1.5.3 and reference_docs/ in README.md:1-50 and SKILL.md:1-24.
records.disposition_rationale: The divergence is in curated documentation, not executable code. The fix is to update the stale bootstrap packet so the self-audit reads current claims.
records.proposed_fix: Refresh the curated bootstrap docs to match the live README/SKILL version and terminology, or label the old framing as historical. See quality/patches/BUG-006-fix.patch.

records.title: Direct-root bootstrap exposes an empty reference_docs tree while harness installs mirror the curated docs set
records.divergence_description: The benchmark harness mirrors docs_gathered/ into reference_docs/, but the repo-root bootstrap run leaves reference_docs/ effectively empty, so Phase 1 sees different documentation trees depending on how QPB is invoked.
records.documented_intent: REQ-008 requires direct-root bootstrap and harness-installed bootstrap to expose the same effective documentation surface to Phase 1.
records.code_behavior: repos/setup_repos.sh:216-220 mirrors docs_gathered/ into reference_docs/ for installed targets, but the repo root currently exposes only reference_docs/.gitkeep and reference_docs/cite/.gitkeep while the curated plaintext bootstrap packet remains in docs_gathered/ (quality/PROGRESS.md:25-28; docs_gathered/INDEX.md:1-37).
records.disposition_rationale: The repo layout and bootstrap path are part of the shipped product surface. Direct-root self-audit should not need a different mental model of where Phase 1 docs live.
records.proposed_fix: Mirror the curated plaintext bootstrap docs into reference_docs/ at the repo root or add bootstrap-only logic that exposes the same effective files through the canonical reference_docs/ path. See quality/patches/BUG-007-fix.patch.
records.consolidation_rationale: All absent cells come from the same missing direct-root mirror: the curated plaintext bootstrap set exists under docs_gathered/ but is not exposed through the canonical reference_docs/ path that the harness populates.
